name: SentinelOne Singularity
description: AI-powered EPP/EDR platform (Singularity). Yields agent deployment and coverage reports, threat detection exports, protection policy configuration, console activity logs, and streamed endpoint telemetry via Cloud Funnel and the Singularity Data Lake.

steps.action: Sign in to the SentinelOne Management Console (https://<your-tenant>.sentinelone.net) with a read-only account
steps.permissions_required: Viewer role (scoped to the relevant account or sites)
steps.security_note: Use the least-privilege Viewer role for evidence collection; do not use an Admin login for read-only tasks. Full administration guides are gated behind the customer Knowledge Base at community.sentinelone.com (login required).

steps.action: Navigate to Sentinels and review the endpoint list; filter by Agent Health Status and Last Active date, then use Actions > Export to download the agent inventory as CSV
steps.audit_note: The agent inventory shows agent version, last active timestamp, network status and disabled/decommissioned state — the primary evidence of endpoint protection coverage

steps.action: Navigate to Incidents (Threats) and set the time filter to the evidence collection period, then export the threat list as CSV
steps.audit_note: Threat records include the AI verdict, analyst verdict, mitigation status and MITRE ATT&CK technique mapping, evidencing detection and response controls

steps.action: Navigate to Sentinels > Policy for each site or group in scope and capture the protection policy settings (Detect vs Protect mode, mitigation actions, agent tamper protection) as a screenshot or PDF print
steps.audit_note: Policy mode evidence demonstrates that prevention (Protect mode) rather than detection-only is enforced on production endpoints

steps.action: Navigate to Activity, filter by activity type (for example user management and policy changes), and export the activity log as CSV
steps.audit_note: The Activity log is the console's audit trail; it records administrative actions with actor and timestamp

steps.action: Upload all exports and screenshots to the evidence repository with evidence ID references and the collection date
In the Management Console, navigate to Reports and create Insight Reports covering threats, mitigation status and endpoint (Sentinel) inventory for the sites in scope
steps.permissions_required: Admin role on the relevant scope
steps.security_note: Report creation requires elevated rights; the detailed report configuration guide is gated behind the customer Knowledge Base at community.sentinelone.com (login required)

steps.action: Set each report to a recurring schedule (weekly or monthly) and add the evidence collection mailbox as an email recipient
steps.audit_note: Vendor-generated scheduled reports carry the generation timestamp and reporting period, strengthening evidentiary value over ad hoc screenshots

steps.action: Scope reports to the specific account, sites or groups that match the audit boundary so coverage figures reconcile with the asset inventory
steps.audit_note: Mis-scoped reports (for example a single site in a multi-site tenant) understate or overstate coverage and undermine the evidence

steps.action: File the delivered report PDFs in the evidence repository per period, and record any weeks where a report failed to arrive
steps.audit_note: A continuous, gap-free series of periodic reports demonstrates sustained monitoring rather than point-in-time compliance

steps.action: Create a dedicated Service User: navigate to Settings > Users > Service Users > Actions > Create New Service User, assign the Viewer role scoped to the sites in scope, and generate its API token
steps.security_note: Store the API token in a secrets manager, never in code or config files. Service user tokens have a fixed expiry set at creation — record the expiry date and rotate before it lapses. Prefer a Service User over a personal user token so collection does not break when staff leave.

steps.action: Implement a daily job calling GET https://<your-tenant>.sentinelone.net/web/api/v2.1/agents with the header 'Authorization: ApiToken <token>' to pull the full agent inventory, following cursor pagination until exhausted
steps.audit_note: Persist the raw JSON with a collection timestamp; agent records include agent version, last active time and infection status for coverage evidence

steps.action: Extend the job to call GET /web/api/v2.1/threats (filtered by createdAt range) for detections and GET /web/api/v2.1/activities for the console audit trail; the full interactive API reference is served from your console at https://<your-tenant>.sentinelone.net/api-doc (login required)
steps.security_note: The complete endpoint reference is gated behind the console API documentation and the customer Knowledge Base; publicly reachable SentinelOne pages describe the platform but not the full API schema

steps.action: Validate each collection run: reconcile the API agent count against the console dashboard figure and alert on discrepancies above a set threshold
steps.audit_note: Automated reconciliation evidences data completeness and detects silently broken collection

steps.action: Add error handling: retry with backoff on HTTP 429 rate-limit responses, and alert the compliance team on authentication failures or token expiry so collection gaps are caught within a day
steps.security_note: Alert on repeated 401 responses — an expired or revoked service user token silently halts evidence collection

L4.title: SentinelOne Managed Endpoint Evidence Pipeline
L4.estimated_time: 4-8 hours initial setup (plus Cloud Funnel licensing)

steps.action: License and enable Singularity Cloud Funnel to stream enriched EDR/XDR telemetry continuously to an organisation-owned Amazon S3 or Google Cloud Storage bucket
steps.permissions_required: Admin role at account level (and an active Cloud Funnel module licence)
steps.security_note: Restrict the destination bucket with least-privilege IAM policies and enable bucket-level encryption and access logging
steps.audit_note: Cloud Funnel delivers compressed JSON telemetry into storage you control, providing an immutable, retained replica of endpoint events independent of console retention limits

steps.action: Use the Singularity Data Lake for long-term hot retention of endpoint telemetry (up to 7 years) and as the query layer for historical evidence requests
steps.audit_note: Long-horizon retention supports auditor requests for historical detection and response records without restoring cold archives

steps.action: Ingest the Cloud Funnel stream (or SIEM forwarding from the Data Lake) into the organisation's SIEM and map detections, mitigations and agent health events to the relevant control evidence requirements

Implement completeness monitoring: reconcile daily agent counts from the Management API against the authoritative asset inventory (CMDB) and alert when unprotected endpoints exceed the agreed threshold
steps.audit_note: Coverage-gap alerting is direct evidence of a managed, self-correcting endpoint protection process

steps.action: Run automated stream health checks — alert if no Cloud Funnel objects land in the destination bucket within the expected interval — and log all gaps with remediation notes
steps.audit_note: Documented gap detection and remediation preserves the evidentiary chain across pipeline outages

steps.action: Generate a monthly endpoint protection evidence pack: coverage percentage, detection and mitigation trends, policy compliance by site, and pipeline completeness metrics
